evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Endpoint places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete with X-Goog-Api-Key header and camelCase body fields (locationBias, includedPrimaryTypes, languageCode, sessionToken) is internally consistent and matches the new Places API v1 surface. No deprecated legacy /maps/api/place/autocomplete/json calls used.
evidence.executability: Fenced blocks parse. Minor issues: npm install commands use `npm install react==18.2.0` (npm syntax is `react@18.2.0` — `==` will fail), and `dotenv` on frontend is unnecessary for CRA. Backend code is complete and importable. No ngrok/sudo/sandbox blockers.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: (a) API key acquisition with console path; (b) pip/npm installs in bash; (c) env var template with GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY and REACT_APP_API_URL; (d) backend main.py; (e) frontend tsx components; (f) pytest + jest test commands. Env vars referenced match those listed.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Multiple canonical developers.google.com/maps URLs cited including the v1 REST reference [10] and place-autocomplete-new [8]; inline [1] markers placed near relevant code. No fabricated URLs.

rubric_notes: npm install syntax with `==` instead of `@` is a real but trivial fix; capped EXECUTABILITY at 4. Docs unavailable: scored on internal consistency, which is high.
